task: Add a small export adapter without changing the existing default path.
headline: The adapter has a separate source contract, rejects malformed rows, and leaves the default export unchanged.
intent: Add one opt-in adapter that downstream consumers can verify without changing the current exporter or treating adapter fields as the canonical source format.

requirements.requirement: The canonical source fields and the adapter-visible fields remain separate contracts.
requirements.why: A downstream serialization shape must not silently become the source-of-truth format.

examples.given: A valid source row contains canonical fields that the adapter renames or derives
examples.then: the source row stays unchanged and the derived fields appear only in adapter output

examples.given: A consumer requests an adapter-only field from the canonical source
examples.then: the request fails instead of widening the source contract

gate.criterion: Given canonical fixtures and their adapter outputs, the source rows remain byte-stable while every derived or renamed field appears only in the adapter artifact.
gate.failure_class: Adapter fields leak into or replace the canonical source contract.
gate.source_of_truth: Canonical fixture rows and the declared adapter-output schema.
gate.input_form: A small fixture set with direct, renamed, and derived fields.
gate.signal: Source fixtures are unchanged, and source-only versus adapter-only fields match the declared schemas exactly.
gate.failure_response: Block the adapter change, keep the canonical source untouched, and repair the source/adapter boundary before retrying.

mutants.mutant: Write a derived adapter field back into the source row.
mutants.hardening: Compare source fixtures before and after adaptation as well as checking output shape.

mutants.mutant: Drop the source schema check and accept an adapter-only field as canonical.
mutants.hardening: Check field membership against both declared schemas.

requirements.requirement: Valid rows produce the declared adapter output, while malformed rows fail before downstream consumption.
requirements.why: A rendered file is not sufficient if masks, coordinates, or required fields can be silently wrong.

examples.given: A valid fixture contains all required fields
examples.then: the output matches the declared schema and expected values

examples.given: A fixture is missing a required field or contains an out-of-range coordinate
examples.then: the row is rejected with a specific validation failure

gate.criterion: Given valid and malformed fixtures, valid outputs exact-match the declared schema and values, and every malformed fixture fails at the contract boundary.
gate.failure_class: Shape-only validation lets semantically invalid adapter rows pass.
gate.source_of_truth: Expected adapter fixtures and explicit invalid-case expectations.
gate.input_form: Valid rows plus missing-field, wrong-mask, and out-of-range-coordinate mutations.
gate.signal: All valid rows exact-match; every invalid row fails for its intended reason before an output artifact is accepted.
gate.failure_response: Reject the adapter artifact, repair the validation or input contract, and rerun the fixture set before downstream use.

mutants.mutant: Keep the right keys but emit an incorrect mask value.
mutants.hardening: Compare values, not only keys and types.

mutants.mutant: Write output before rejecting an out-of-range coordinate.
mutants.hardening: Require validation to finish before the artifact is accepted.

requirements.requirement: The adapter remains opt-in, and the existing default export stays unchanged on the same fixtures.
requirements.why: Adding the adapter must not widen the rollout or change existing consumers without a separate decision.

examples.when: the existing export entrypoint runs on the fixture set
examples.then: its output is byte-for-byte identical to the baseline
only the adapter artifact is added and the default artifact stays unchanged

gate.criterion: Given the same fixture set, the default command exact-matches its baseline output, while the explicit adapter path adds only the declared adapter artifact.
gate.failure_class: The new adapter changes the default path or existing output as a side effect.
gate.source_of_truth: A frozen default-output baseline and the adapter artifact contract.
gate.input_form: The same fixtures run once without the adapter and once with it explicitly enabled.
gate.signal: Default output exact-matches the baseline; the opt-in run adds one valid adapter artifact and changes nothing else.
gate.failure_response: Block rollout, restore the unchanged default path, and investigate the compatibility drift before retrying.

mutants.mutant: Enable the adapter by default while keeping the old artifact present.
mutants.hardening: Run the entrypoint without any new flag and compare the complete output set.

mutants.mutant: Change whitespace or field ordering in the default artifact.
mutants.hardening: Use a byte-for-byte baseline for the unchanged default path.

scopes.name: Adapter contract
scopes.outcome: Source data remains canonical, and adapter output is both exact and fail-fast.
rabbit_holes: Treating an output schema as the canonical source format
Checking only JSON shape instead of model-visible values

scopes.name: Default-path compatibility
scopes.outcome: Existing consumers see no change unless the adapter is explicitly enabled.
rabbit_holes: Using a successful adapter artifact as proof that the default path stayed unchanged

decisions.decision: Should a later release make the adapter the default?

options.label: A
options.shape: Keep it opt-in until consumers explicitly migrate.
options.implication: This change carries no default-path migration.

options.label: B
options.shape: Make it the default in this change.
options.implication: This requires a separate compatibility and rollout contract.

decisions.reason: The current goal is an additive adapter with a bounded blast radius.

non_goals: Running a full dataset export before the fixture gates pass.
Removing the existing output format in this change.
